evidence: 21 test classes found: api/ (HandlersTest, HttpApiUtilsTest, PatternSubstitutionTest, RelationUtilsTest), config/ (GrammarConfigLoaderTest, RelationsValidationTest), exploration/ (AdjectiveCollocateRankerTest), query/ (BCQLParserTest, BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest, BlackLabDummyTest, BlackLabQueryExecutorTest, BlackLabSnippetParserTest, CollocateQueryHelperTest, DependencySketchTest, HybridAlgorithmTest, SemanticFieldExplorerTest), utils/ (CqlUtilsTest, LogDiceCalculatorTest, LongIntHashMapTest), viz/ (RadialPlotTest)
BlackLabConllUIndexer.java (indexer/blacklab/) has no corresponding test class — the indexer is a critical path for corpus ingestion and has no unit or integration test
ConlluConverter.java has no corresponding test class — conversion of CoNLL-U to WPL chunks is critical for correct index construction; errors here would silently produce wrong index content
WordSketchApiServer.java has no direct test — the server startup, route registration, and CORS handling are only tested indirectly through handler tests
HandlersTest exists (api/) but likely tests handler logic in isolation via mocks; no end-to-end HTTP test found (no e2e/ test using a real HTTP client against a live server port)
BlackLabQueryExecutorTest exists but likely requires a real BlackLab index to run; a dummy index test (BlackLabDummyTest) suggests some index-based tests are separated
SemanticFieldExplorerTest and AdjectiveCollocateRankerTest cover the exploration logic with presumably mock executors
No test for the ConlluConverter chunk-boundary logic (what happens when a sentence spans a chunk boundary), which is a tricky edge case

strengths: 21 test classes with broad coverage of query, exploration, config, and utility logic
Unit tests exist for all utility classes (CqlUtilsTest, LogDiceCalculatorTest, LongIntHashMapTest) — pure-logic code is well-covered
BlackLabSnippetParserTest covers the XML parsing that is central to collocate extraction — high-value test for a brittle parsing layer
RelationsValidationTest provides schema-level validation of the grammar config, acting as an integration guard for configuration changes
test_strategy.issue_character: The indexer pipeline (BlackLabConllUIndexer + ConlluConverter) — which is the entry point for all corpus data and the source of index correctness — has zero test coverage, while all downstream query logic is tested; this is an inverted risk profile.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The test suite is strong for query, exploration, and config logic, but the zero coverage of the indexer pipeline is a critical gap: bugs in ConlluConverter or BlackLabConllUIndexer would silently produce a corrupt index, causing query results to be wrong without any test failure. This gap plus the absence of any end-to-end HTTP test pushes the score to 62 — well below what the quantity of test classes alone would suggest.

issues.summary: ConlluConverter (CoNLL-U to WPL chunk conversion) has no unit tests despite being a critical indexing step
evidence: No ConlluConverterTest class found in any test directory
ConlluConverter.convertConlluToWplChunks handles multi-word token filtering (MWT_OR_EMPTY pattern), sentence boundary detection, chunk splitting, and file writing — each is a distinct edge case
Critical edge case: what happens when a sentence is split across a chunk boundary (sentencesPerChunk boundary hits mid-sentence)? The implementation uses inSentence flag — untested
issues.suggestion: Add ConlluConverterTest with cases: (1) basic sentence conversion, (2) MWT line filtering (e.g., '1-2' and '2.1' tokens), (3) chunk boundary: verify last sentence of chunk N appears in chunk N and first sentence of chunk N+1 appears in next chunk, (4) empty input, (5) input with only comments. Use an in-memory temp directory for output.

issues.summary: BlackLabConllUIndexer has no unit or integration test
evidence: BlackLabConllUIndexer opens a BlackLabIndexWriter and registers error-continuation behavior (errorOccurred returns true) — this means indexing errors are swallowed silently and logged; no test verifies that error documents are counted or that the index is still valid
The indexer is the critical first step before any query; a regression here would produce an empty or corrupt index with no test failure
issues.suggestion: Add a BlackLabConllUIndexerTest that creates a temporary index, indexes a small 3-sentence CoNLL-U sample (could be from test-data/ directory), and verifies that the resulting index has the expected document count. This requires a BlackLab test dependency but BlackLabDummyTest and BlackLabQueryExecutorTest already show this pattern is in use.

issues.summary: No end-to-end HTTP test verifies the full request/response cycle through WordSketchApiServer
evidence: WordSketchApiServer itself has no test class
HandlersTest likely tests handlers directly (as Java method calls), not via HTTP
CORS preflight handling, 405 Method Not Allowed enforcement, and route path matching are implemented in WordSketchApiServer.registerHandler — none of these are reachable through handler-level tests
issues.suggestion: Add a WordSketchApiServerIntegrationTest that starts the server on a random port with a test index and a test grammar config, then sends real HTTP requests using HttpClient (Java 11+) to verify: (1) /health returns 200 with {status:ok}, (2) OPTIONS /api/sketch/test returns 204 with CORS headers, (3) POST to a GET-only endpoint returns 405.

root_causes: Indexer code was likely written once and treated as infrastructure-level; its critical nature for test data quality was not reflected in test investment
Handler-level mocking provides a false sense of API coverage without exercising the HTTP plumbing
likely_symptoms: conllu_converter_untested is highest priority — silent data corruption in the indexing pipeline affects all downstream query tests
